reasoning: Company homepage and about pages provided the official name, broad description, and ownership. Product pages supplied core offering names and product-specific claims. LinkedIn provided employee band, founding year, and revenue band. Company news releases and BusinessWire were used for recent M&A and product-integration signals. Where metrics appear on multiple pages, the phrasing was preserved in notableMetrics and noted in researchNotes.

coreOfferings.name: DAT One / Load Board
coreOfferings.description: Truckload load board and end-to-end freight marketplace for carriers, brokers, shippers and 3PLs.

coreOfferings.name: RateView / RateView Analytics
coreOfferings.description: Industry-standard truckload pricing and forecasting analytics for spot and contract rates.

coreOfferings.name: DAT iQ / Benchmark & Analytics Services
coreOfferings.description: Transportation market intelligence, benchmarking, forecasting, and custom analytics services.

coreOfferings.name: Convoy Platform (freight-matching automation)
coreOfferings.description: Automated freight-matching platform integrated into DATs marketplace and TMS offerings (Convoy Platform integration/acquisition referenced in news).

coreOfferings.name: Outgo (factoring & payments)
coreOfferings.description: Integrated factoring and payments platform for carriers with fast funding and rate-match guarantee.

recentSignals.signal: DAT acquired Outgo, adding integrated factoring/payments; Outgo integrates with DAT One.
DAT announced intent to acquire the Convoy Platform from Flexport.
Convoy Platform made available free to DAT and BrokerPro TMS customers (integration/expansion signal).

researchNotes: Primary sources: DAT corporate site (products/about/news) and LinkedIn. Recent signals (Outgo acquisition, Convoy Platform acquisition and integration) come from DAT news releases and BusinessWire. Some internal metric phrasing varies between pages (e.g., '150,000+ transactions per minute' vs. '266 million transactions'); metrics are company-published.
oneLineDescription: Operator of North Americas largest truckload freight marketplace and freight analytics.

companyScaleSignals.ownership: Business unit / subsidiary of Roper Technologies (public)
notableMetrics: database exceeding $1 trillion in freight-market transactions
266 million transactions across all lanes (annual figure cited on site)
more than 150,000 transactions per minute (load board claim)

positioningParagraph: DAT provides an end-to-end truckload marketplace and transportation analytics for motor carriers, freight brokers, shippers and 3PLs, solving load matching, pricing, visibility, and payment friction. Its differentiators are a massive proprietary transaction dataset, broad network scale, integrated visibility/payments, and ownership by Roper Technologies.

targetCustomerProfile.primary: freight brokers and motor carriers (including owner-operators)
targetCustomerProfile.sizeSegment: SMB to enterprise (owner-operators through large shippers/3PLs)

partnershipAppetiteHints: Integrated load tracking via Trucker Tools
Factoring product partnership (Outgo) and broker-approved factorable loads
Analytics services offering API integrations and custom data services
competitiveLandscapePosition: Market-leading freight marketplace and analytics provider in North America; differentiates on unmatched data scale, rate accuracy, integrated visibility, and payments.
